title_original: Rare earth barium copper oxide magnet coils and methods
abstract_snippet_original: REBCO tape with thin resistive coating layers (copper oxide, Cr, Ni, or Ni-P) and REBCO tapes with stainless-steel interlayers (Ni or Cu plated) to control turn-to-turn contact resistivity (Rc); thermal cycling 4.2K->273K increases Rc from ~0.8 to ~3.1 microOhm-cm^2 (does not reset to as-built value); mechanical/pressure cycling (~1000 cycles, 2.5-25 MPa) decreases Rc to ~1/10 of initial value.
assignee: Florida State University Research Foundation, Inc.
relevance_sentence: Already claims engineered resistive/interlayer coatings to control turn-to-turn Rc and reports both thermal-cycling and mechanical-cycling Rc-degradation data, directly overlapping CF-6's target phenomenon though not framed as CTE-matching or as a cryogen-free-vs-bath comparison.
query_terms_that_found_it: CTE matched interlayer no-insulation HTS coil conduction cooled patent
SuperPower no-insulation REBCO coil patent US

title_original: Feedback control for no-insulation high-temperature superconducting magnet
abstract_snippet_original: Active feedback controller for NI HTS magnet power supply to reduce/eliminate charging delay and linearize magnet constant; models Rc as lumped parallel resistor (~3.5 mOhm estimated); tested at 77K with design consideration for 4.2K.
assignee: Florida State University Research Foundation, Inc.
relevance_sentence: Same assignee/family as CF6-T1-1; controls Rc effects via feedback rather than interface material engineering, lower direct overlap but included for family/assignee-watch candor.
query_terms_that_found_it: SuperPower no-insulation REBCO coil patent US

title_original: Partially-insulated HTS coils
abstract_snippet_original: Metal-insulator-transition material (e.g. vanadium oxide) between turns with resistance changing >=10x around ~110K, giving temperature-dependent turn-to-turn Rc control distinct from cooldown/cycling-fatigue-dependent control.
relevance_sentence: Same problem space (turn-to-turn Rc engineering) via a phase-transition-material mechanism rather than CTE-matched/compliant interlayer; adjacent but not anticipating art, must remain in IDS_pool.
query_terms_that_found_it: CTE matched interlayer no-insulation HTS coil conduction cooled patent

title_translated: Electrically-conductive-material-impregnated no-insulation superconducting coil and manufacturing apparatus thereof
abstract_snippet_original: 무절연 초전도 코일 권선시 도전성 입자를 함침하여 턴간 저항을 유지하면서 기계적 강도를 보강 (per fetched summary).
abstract_snippet_translated: Impregnates conductive particles (metallic/carbon, polymer matrix, optionally conductive balls sized ~sqrt(3)x wire radius) into the NI coil during winding to reinforce mechanical integrity while keeping turn-to-turn resistance in an intermediate (non-insulating) range and spreading hot-spot current across turns.
status: granted (per Google Patents; live KIPRIS status not independently confirmed)
relevance_sentence: Addresses mechanical robustness of the NI interface via conductive-composite fill, adjacent to but materially different from a CTE-matched or spring-loaded compliant interlayer, and does not explicitly address thermal-cycling fatigue.

title_original: Cryocooler cold head interface receptacle
abstract_snippet_original: Belleville-washer-loaded cold-head interface maintaining/increasing contact pressure at a cryocooler cold-stage joint (interface pressure up to ~468 psi; pressure tunable via bellows diameter/spring constant).
relevance_sentence: General mechanical precedent for a spring-loaded contact maintaining pressure across cooldown at a cryocooler cold-head interface; anticipates the generic mechanical concept CF-6's spring-loaded-contact embodiment would need to differentiate from, though not HTS-turn-to-turn-specific.

title_original: 高温超导体场线圈
abstract_snippet_original: 通过在线圈绕制后对HTS带材轴向边缘进行材料去除（机械加工/放电加工/激光切割或化学方法），缩减带材轴向范围并改变绕组间电气特性，暴露缓冲层作为匈间绝缘（'缓冲层绝缘'，BLI），无需额外绝缘材料即可实现匈间绝缘/电气特性调节。

title_original: Feasibility Study of the Impregnation of a No-Insulation HTS Coil Using an Electrically Conductive Epoxy
abstract_snippet_original: Full-surface epoxy impregnation across turn-to-turn contacts caused significant critical-current degradation from delamination driven by thermal-contraction mismatch between the epoxy composite and the HTS tape; a partial-depth impregnation method (epoxy penetrates only part-way between turns) was developed to reduce this mismatch-driven degradation.
number: Cryogenics journal (ResearchGate mirror 313682731); exact DOI not independently confirmed this session
assignee: MIT Francis Bitter Magnet Lab / Iwasa-Hahn NI-coil group lineage (attribution per assignee-watch prior knowledge, author list not independently re-confirmed this session)
relevance_sentence: Published, named instance of exactly the CTE-mismatch delamination failure mode CF-6 targets, and already discloses a partial-depth-impregnation design-around; leading candidate blocking/limiting reference at G-NOVEL.
